etymology.description: semantic 日 (rì, "sun") + phonetic 乍 (zhà) -> yesterday
etymology.interpretation: The character 昨 (zuó, "yesterday") combines the semantic component 日 (rì, "sun"), representing the day, with the phonetic component 乍 (zhà), which provides the sound. The combination intuitively indicates the concept of 'yesterday,' which is the day that has passed.

examples.chinese: 昨天我和朋友去看电影(昨天我和朋友去看電影)。
examples.pinyin: Zuótiān wǒ hé péngyǒu qù kàn diànyǐng.
examples.english: Yesterday, my friend and I went to watch a movie.

examples.chinese: 他昨天做了什么(他昨天做了什麼)？
昨天下了雨(昨天下了雨)，所以路上很湿(所以路上很濕)。
examples.pinyin: Zuótiān xiàle yǔ, suǒyǐ lùshàng hěn shī.
examples.english: It rained yesterday, so the roads are very wet.
